description: Log cron-runtime job errors (ERR_JOB) instead of silently swallowing them — replace croner's boolean catch:true with a named, injectable onJobError handler that records an ERR_JOB line to crons/.cron.log, preserving crash-resistance.

userStories.title: Replace silent catch:true with onJobError handler
userStories.description: As a harness operator, I want a cron job-callback error recorded so a failed cron is distinguishable from an idle one in crons/.cron.log.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/cron-runtime.ts defines and exports onJobError(id: string, err: unknown, logFn = log): void that calls logFn(id, "ERR_JOB", String(err)); logFn defaults to the internal log helper and exists only for test injection.
The module-private log helper is NOT exported; only onJobError becomes a new export. Existing internal log call sites unchanged.
Line 180 new Cron(...) replaces `catch: true` with `catch: (err: unknown) => onJobError(e.id, err)` closing over e.id; timezone and protect options unchanged.
onJobError fires only on a synchronous job-callback throw; overlap handling unchanged (a skipped fire still logs SKIPPED_OVERLAP, never ERR_JOB).
No `catch: true` literal remains: grep -n "catch: true" scripts/cron-runtime.ts returns nothing.

userStories.title: Deterministic unit test for onJobError
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a deterministic test for onJobError so the ERR_JOB logging contract is regression-protected.
acceptanceCriteria: A new describe("onJobError") block in scripts/__tests__/cron-runtime.test.ts calls onJobError("testjob", new Error("disk full"), spy) with an injected spy logger and asserts the spy received exactly ("testjob", "ERR_JOB", "Error: disk full").
Test is deterministic and touches no filesystem; it must not rely on setting process.env.CRONS_DIR after the top-level import (LOG_FILE is captured at module load).
onJobError is added to the destructured import from ../cron-runtime; existing imports preserved.
All existing tests still pass; the run does not write to the repo's real crons/.cron.log.

userStories.title: Document the ERR_JOB status token
userStories.description: As a reader of the cron runtime, I want ERR_JOB documented so the new log lines are self-explanatory.
acceptanceCriteria: crons/README.md gains a concise status-token list (e.g. a `## Status tokens` section) enumerating SPAWNED, FIRE, OK, EXIT_n, ERR, ERR_JOB, SKIPPED_OVERLAP, BOOT, each with a one-line meaning.
ERR_JOB is described as firing when a synchronous cron job-callback throws, with log format `<id>\tERR_JOB\t<error-string>`.
grep -n "ERR_JOB" crons/README.md returns a hit; existing prose/sections are additive-only (not deleted).
